paragraphs.body: Ryan M. Owen Braids: Entertainment, Friendship, Anxiety An Essay on Me: In Alphabetical Order Anything When you have General Anxiety disorder, you overthink and stress over almost anything and everything. The best way to tune out the stress is to focus on breathing, counting, or exercising.

Bowling Bowling is one of my favorite pastimes because I can just relax and enjoy myself with my friends. Joining my high school was a great decision because I was able to chill after school and enjoy a sport with some friends. College College has caused a complete brain spiral because of its intricacies. Last semester, I had tons of free time, but little to no free time this semester due to rushing. It’s crazy how fast my life and routine can change due to the introduction of some aspects of my life.

Don’t Worry : By Ray Dalton and Madcon Whenever I feel stressed, I turn on this song to ease the nerves and calm myself down. When I first discovered this song, it had no meaning to me, but once I learned the lyrics and memorized the catchy tune, it stuck with me and I applied it to my life for the better.

Exercise Since coming back from winter break, I have exercised everyday whether it’s going on walks, working out, or even just doing a few push-ups in my room. Exercise helps activate the anxiety releasing neurotransmitters that help me calm down.

Fraternity Although stressful at times, joining a fraternity has been my best decision so far this semester because I have made so many more friends than I expected. I’ve probably made stronger and more firm connections than I did with anyone last semester.

Gucci On the first day of rushing, I wore a Gucci tie to an event and they really respected my willingness to wear it even though it might get dirty. That moment helped me realize a lot of the brothers respected me even though it was such a small detail.

Happiness Overall, my level of happiness has shifted throughout the last year. Before I discovered my anxiety problems, I was just a clueless boy. But after, I became way more aware of what changes I needed to make in my lifestyle to cater to my problems and help me become a healthy person again.

annotations.text: anxiety problems, I was just a clueless boy. But after, I became way more aware of what changes I needed to make in my lifestyle to
annotations.comment: I love that you are balancing anxiety and joy throughout this essay, but here is a moment when I think you could find some concrete and specific details to strengthen the writing.

paragraphs.body: Individualism I have come to realize that some people don’t understand what I’m going through and think it’s just a simple little problem when in reality it’s a true psychological disorder. Additionally, some people can’t accept the fact that my anxiety might be worse than theirs even though it is. My best response to that is just a simple “OK” and walk away.

Gianna My brother has a Twitter account with 176k followers where he posts funny baseball content whether it’s players failing or unsuccessful predictions from reporters. I get a real laugh out of some of his content on the daily. I’m looking forward to more posts since baseball season is about to start.

annotations.text: My brother has a Twitter account with 176k followers where he posts funny baseball content whether it’s players failing or unsuccessful predictions from reporters. I get a real laugh out of some of his content on the daily. I’m looking forward to more posts since baseball season is about to start.
annotations.comment: This is some of the clearest, most specific writing in the piece. It's really working well.

paragraphs.body: Kate My sister has been a great help in helping my anxiety go away because she has dealt with the exact same thing. I didn’t even know she did which helped me realize it’s not as big of a problem as I might think. Additionally, she is one of the most naturally funny people I’ve ever met and keeps me entertained every time I spend time with her.

Laughing Being able to have a good laugh shows me that I want to spend more time with you because I enjoy myself the most when I have a good laugh. A lot of my new friends in my pledge class make me laugh so much and that’s why I’m so excited to get initiated.

Mom My mom has been the biggest help in dealing with my anxiety. She has also dealt with the exact same thing and showed me how common and non-threatening it actually is. When I first discovered I had GAD, she was right by my side for 4 days straight, every step of the way to make sure I was okay. Her being there for me helped me realize that I’m not alone and I always have people who have my back. Additionally, she influenced me to help other people dealing with anxiety or other problems like depression to pay it forward.
Nate One of my best friends Nate is a guy I met less than 2 months ago. It’s crazy to think how quickly we can make connections with other people and how well I know them.

Orioles With baseball season starting up again, I look forward to being able to watch my favorite team, the Baltimore Orioles, because they’re one of the most entertaining teams in the MLB. Last season, they hit the most homeruns in the league.

Pittsburgh Coming to Pittsburgh was a major change from my suburban area in Long Island. Although it was a very stressful transition, I’ve adapted really well and absolutely love where I decided to go. I wouldn’t change my decision if I could.

Quiplash Quiplash is a game I play with my friends all the time to have a good time and get a good laugh out of each other. Even if you don’t know the people you’re playing with, it’s still so much fun to play.

Ryan I am myself and no one else is me. I think about this sentence all the time and although it is a bit stressful to think about, it tells myself that no one controls what I do other than myself, so I should live young, wild, and free.

annotations.text: I am myself and no one else is me. I think about this sentence all the time and although it is a bit stressful to think about, it tells myself that no one controls what I do other than myself, so I should live young, wild, and free.
annotations.comment: This is wonderful. I think this moment points to your essay's central focusing idea.

paragraphs.body: Sports I can’t imagine a world where sports don’t exist. They are by far the most entertaining aspect of the lives of 90% of men including myself. Without sports, I don’t know how bored I would be on a day to day basis.

Time When dealing with stress and anxiety, it’s hard to keep track of time because it feels like it goes by so slow.My best solution for this is to refrain from looking at the clock and just live in the moment because I enjoy myself when I don’t think about externalities.

Ultimate Team One of my favorite things to do in my free time is play Ultimate Team FIFA with my friend Rees. Although I destroy him every time, I know we both still have a good time because we fool around and have a bunch of laughs.

Vision Anxiety causes you to have unlimited visual thoughts through your brain at all times. Although I see myself as a very introspective person, my vision has always stuck with me and I have such an easy time remembering them. I think sometimes I have a photographic memory.

PS. One of my friends has a photographic memory in which he can memorize anyones license plate and it’s one of the craziest things I’ve ever seen Will Siegel Will is my roommate who I deem as my best friend. He's been there for me through tough times for the past 8 months whether it's through anxiety or rushing. We do almost everything together and I look forward to spending many years with him.
X-Factor Although it seems like I’ve found things to stop my anxiety, I’m still looking for the X-factor that stops it and still allows me to do all of the things I love.

YouTube Some of my favorite times are scrolling through YouTube and watching completely random videos ranging from “Top 10 Bench Clearing Brawls” to “ Building an Underwater Tunnel”. YouTube has literally everything you can think of.

ZZZ Because I have anxiety, I tend to have trouble sleeping at night because I can’t sit still or my mind is racing. I never get a good night's sleep and always take naps. My best solution is listening to calming voices while I sleep or meditating before I go to bed to tense my muscles and make myself tired.

annotations.comment: Hello, I really enjoyed getting to know you even better through reading this very personal essay. One of the things that really stood out to me the most was your focus throughout the piece on your feelings of anxiety—that is clearly the focusing idea of your essay. You do a great job of using your big ideas—friends, family, and sports—to build a complete narrative which always touches on anxiety, and, yet, each of these big ideas has its own place in the writing. Some of the words that stood out to me the most were the names that you included. For instance, rather than using B for Brother, S for Sister, or R for Roommate, you named each of these people, which made me feel as though they are much more than just generic titles, but real and important people to you. The M for Mom worked just as well, since most of us do not call our parents by their proper name, and I felt the connection between you and her, because, again, you’ve done such a great job of focusing on the anxiety that you and she share and tying the big ideas together thematically. While this is not always the case, I feel as though the audience for this piece is often the people you are writing about—your friends, family, and, honestly, yourself. You do a wonderful job of focusing on your anxiety while not complaining about it, and, in doing so, you are celebrating the people you love, the people who bring you joy, the people who help you find your happiness. This is an essay of gratitude to and for those people—its purpose is to celebrate their involvement in your life. I have heard it said that each of us is a combination of the five people we spend the most time with in our lives. One of the things I learned from reading your essay is that you seem to be building a community of people that extends beyond five, and each of these people is a huge part of who you are as an individual. You’ve done a great job of showing your reader this. It was an absolute pleasure getting to know those folks, and, of course, you better through reading this piece. Thank You,
